name: Macaroni and cheese (food medium)
matrix_note: Mixed cooked dish = durum-wheat macaroni + dairy cheese sauce (cheese, milk, butter). USDA reference: SR Legacy 169770 'Macaroni and cheese, box mix with cheese sauce, prepared'. Dominant carbon/energy source is STARCH from the pasta; secondary fermentable carbon is lactose from the dairy fraction plus lactic/citric acid from the cheese.

components.name: starch
components.evidence: Dominant carbohydrate/carbon source of the dish; gelatinised durum-wheat starch from cooked macaroni.

components.name: lactose
components.evidence: Only significant free disaccharide; from milk/cheese dairy fraction (C12H22O11).

components.name: maltose
components.evidence: From partial starch hydrolysis during cooking.

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Below detection in the finished dish (bound as starch/lactose).

components.name: lactic acid
components.source: ESTIMATE: cheddar 1.44 g/100g (HPLC, PMC10171539) x dairy/cheese fraction of the dish
components.evidence: Principal organic acid, entirely missing from the FooDB record; produced by lactic-acid bacteria converting lactose during cheese ripening. Amount is an order-of-magnitude estimate scaled from cheddar to the dish's cheese fraction (calcium 85 vs cheddar ~720 mg/100g => ~10-15% cheese-equivalent).

components.name: citric acid
components.source: ESTIMATE: natural cheese 0.056 g/100g (PMC10171539) + citric acid/citrate added to box cheese-sauce mix (food-science ingredient lists)
components.evidence: Missing from FooDB record. Box mac-&-cheese sauce powders add citric acid and sodium citrate as acidulant/emulsifier, so total exceeds the natural dairy level; flagged estimate.

components.name: acetic acid
components.source: ESTIMATE: cheddar 0.23 g/100g (PMC10171539) x cheese fraction
components.evidence: Minor organic acid from cheese; estimate.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Wheat gluten (Glu/Gln- and Pro-rich) + milk casein (Glu/Pro-rich). USDA 169770 gives no measured free-amino-acid panel, so individual AAs below are protein-bound and unquantified.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Dominant residue of both gluten and casein; amount not quantified in USDA 169770.

components.name: L-proline
components.evidence: High in gluten and casein; unquantified.

components.name: L-lysine
components.evidence: Limiting AA of wheat, supplemented by dairy casein.

components.name: L-valine
components.source: protein-derived (added; absent from FooDB current list)
components.evidence: Essential AA present in gluten+casein; USDA gives no amount.

components.name: L-phenylalanine
components.source: protein-derived (added; absent from FooDB current list)
components.evidence: Essential AA; USDA gives no amount.

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: Protein serine is the L-enantiomer; FooDB's D-Serine is a stereochemistry error.

components.name: polyunsaturated fatty acids
components.evidence: Mainly linoleic (C18:2), with trace alpha-linolenic (C18:3 n-3).

components.name: cholesterol
components.evidence: REAL here (animal/dairy dish): measured 8 mg/100g. Not FooDB noise for this food.

components.name: butanoic acid (C4:0)
components.evidence: Characteristic short-chain milkfat FA; present, unquantified.

components.name: pentadecanoic acid (C15:0)
components.evidence: Odd-chain FA, genuine ruminant-dairy biomarker; keep.

components.name: heptadecanoic / heptanoic FA
components.evidence: Trace odd-chain dairy FA; present, unquantified.

components.name: vitamin A
components.evidence: Retinol from dairy + beta-carotene from cheese colourant/annatto.

components.name: folate (total)
components.evidence: Present (enriched flour typically adds folic acid) but not quantified in this USDA entry.

components.name: vitamin C
components.evidence: Effectively absent in a cooked box mix; not quantified.

components.name: vitamin D
components.evidence: Trace from fortified milk; not quantified.

components.name: vitamin K
components.evidence: Menaquinone (MK-4) from dairy fat plausible; not quantified.

components.name: choline
components.evidence: Present (dairy + wheat) but not quantified in this entry.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: From enriched wheat flour. Consolidates FooDB's triplicate Fe entries.

components.name: selenium
components.evidence: Present (wheat) but not quantified in this entry.

remove.name: CO2 CO2
remove.reason: Metabolic gas / model exchange metabolite, not a food nutrient of the dish.

remove.name: O2 O2
remove.reason: Model exchange metabolite (dissolved O2), not a food nutrient.

remove.name: H+
remove.reason: Proton / model artifact, not a nutrient.

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: Free cobalt ion, ultratrace FooDB/model artifact; cobalt is nutritionally relevant only bound in vitamin B12, which is listed separately.

remove.name: Ammonium
remove.reason: FooDB/model nitrogen artifact, not a defined nutrient of macaroni and cheese.

remove.name: Molybdate
remove.reason: Ultratrace, unquantified; negligible growth relevance in this matrix (FooDB noise).

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: Duplicate iron speciation; consolidated into single 'iron' (1.17 mg/100g).

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: Model compartment/speciation duplicate of iron; consolidated into 'iron'.

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: Stereochemistry error; protein serine is L-serine (added under correct name).

remove.name: Eicosapentaenoic acid
remove.reason: Marine omega-3 (EPA); biologically implausible in a wheat+dairy dish with no fish; FooDB predicted noise.

remove.name: Doconexent
remove.reason: DHA, marine omega-3; implausible in macaroni and cheese; FooDB predicted noise.

remove.name: Gamolenic acid
remove.reason: gamma-Linolenic acid; negligible/implausible in dairy+wheat fat; FooDB predicted noise.

missing_added: starch 19.4 g/100g (dominant carbon source, was absent as a quantified nutrient)
individual sugars with amounts: lactose 1.3, maltose 0.27, sucrose 0.01 g/100g
organic acids (entirely absent from FooDB): lactic ~0.2, citric ~0.1, acetic ~0.03 g/100g
L-valine and L-phenylalanine (missing from FooDB amino-acid list)
quantified vitamins (A, B1, B2, B3, B5, B6, B12, E) and minerals (Na, P, Ca, K, Mg, Fe, Zn, Mn, Cu)
keep_uncovered: menatetrenone / MK-4 and phytomenadione (vitamin K vitamers, plausible dairy trace)
retinol and choline (genuine, listed with null where unquantified)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 169770 (SR Legacy: 'Macaroni and cheese, box mix with cheese sauce, prepared') - full nutrient panel via nutritionvalue.org and myfooddata.com mirrors
HPLC measurement of carbohydrates and organic acids in cheese varieties (cheddar: lactic 1.44, citric 0.056, acetic 0.23, succinic 0.01, butanoic 0.15 g/100g), PMC10171539
Food-science ingredient analyses of box cheese-sauce mixes (added citric acid + sodium citrate/phosphate acidulant-emulsifiers)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy source is STARCH (19.4 g/100g) from the pasta; secondary fermentable carbon is lactose (1.3 g/100g) plus cheese-derived lactic and citric acid, which were entirely missing from the FooDB record. Organic-acid amounts are order-of-magnitude estimates scaled from cheddar HPLC data to the dish's cheese fraction (flagged). USDA 169770 provides no measured free-amino-acid profile and no vitamin C/D/K/folate/choline/selenium values, so those are listed present with null amounts. Cholesterol is genuine here (animal/dairy dish) and was NOT removed; removed items are model/exchange ions, duplicate iron speciation, a D-serine stereo error, and implausible marine/GLA fatty acids.
